project.description: Ship a reliable v1 of Data Workbench where humans and agents share one governed source of truth for graph structure, execution state, reviews, briefs, and persistence, with a maintainable codebase and a clear operator workflow.
project.name: Data Workbench

summary: Generated 13 recovery work items from `plans/data-workbench/revisions/r005-approved-plan.md` using the bring-it-home workstreams.

tasks.description:
This milestone groups the work needed to `finish the shippable core`.

### Why this milestone exists
Ship a reliable v1 of Data Workbench where humans and agents share one governed source of truth for graph structure, execution state, reviews, briefs, and persistence, with a maintainable codebase and a clear operator workflow.

### PM expectation
- use this milestone to track the health of the whole workstream, not just one engineering change
- child tasks should make the milestone understandable even for someone reading Notion without the repo open
tasks.title: Finish the shippable core

tasks.description:
This `Task` belongs to the `Finish the shippable core` workstream.

### Why this matters
Keep the graph as structural source of truth, structure bundles/reviews as proposal truth, and `plan_state` as execution truth, matching the operator guide.

### Project goal alignment
Ship a reliable v1 of Data Workbench where humans and agents share one governed source of truth for graph structure, execution state, reviews, briefs, and persistence, with a maintainable codebase and a clear operator workflow.

### Done means
- README, operator guide, runtime plans, and UI/API behavior describe the same day-to-day workflow
- a human PM can understand what this area does without opening the repo first
tasks.title: Keep graph, reviews, and plan_state roles clear

tasks.description:
This `Task` belongs to the `Finish the shippable core` workstream.

### Why this matters
Finish and stabilize the active execution-state surface: source-of-truth payloads, execution endpoints, agent contracts/briefs, execution panel UX, and the supporting persistence layer.

### Project goal alignment
Ship a reliable v1 of Data Workbench where humans and agents share one governed source of truth for graph structure, execution state, reviews, briefs, and persistence, with a maintainable codebase and a clear operator workflow.

### Done means
- payloads, endpoints, briefs, UI state, and persistence behavior all agree on the same execution-state contract
- operators and agents can inspect or update execution state without guessing which surface is authoritative
tasks.title: Stabilize execution-state surface

tasks.description:
This `Task` belongs to the `Finish the shippable core` workstream.

### Why this matters
Preserve the canonical local run path (`PYTHONPATH=src python -m workbench.app`) and the Docker Compose path with app, worker, Postgres, and MinIO.

### Project goal alignment
Ship a reliable v1 of Data Workbench where humans and agents share one governed source of truth for graph structure, execution state, reviews, briefs, and persistence, with a maintainable codebase and a clear operator workflow.

### Done means
- the canonical commands and persisted artifacts are documented, reproducible, and easy to inspect locally
- future contributors can tell which runtime outputs are source-of-truth, generated evidence, or disposable local noise
tasks.title: Preserve canonical run paths

tasks.description:
This `Task` belongs to the `Finish the shippable core` workstream.

### Why this matters
Keep unit, API, persistence, and browser E2E coverage green for the v1 surface that is being shipped.

### Project goal alignment
Ship a reliable v1 of Data Workbench where humans and agents share one governed source of truth for graph structure, execution state, reviews, briefs, and persistence, with a maintainable codebase and a clear operator workflow.

### Done means
- automated coverage exists for the real shipped workflow, not only prototype behavior
- regressions in this area fail loudly enough that future maintenance stays safe
tasks.title: Keep v1 test coverage green

tasks.title: Keep one canonical product story

tasks.description:
This `Task` belongs to the `Keep the project maintainable` workstream.

### Why this matters
Reduce coupling inside large files such as `src/workbench/app.py` and `static/js/app.js` so future updates stay reviewable.

### Project goal alignment
Ship a reliable v1 of Data Workbench where humans and agents share one governed source of truth for graph structure, execution state, reviews, briefs, and persistence, with a maintainable codebase and a clear operator workflow.

### Done means
- the boundary for this work is explicit enough that future changes remain reviewable
- old or prototype surfaces no longer compete with the maintained v1 path
tasks.title: Reduce coupling in large application files

### Why this matters

### Project goal alignment

### Done means

tasks.title: Separate source-of-truth artifacts from local noise
